evals.prompt: Protect my Grafana instance with hlab-auth. I already run Traefik in front of `grafana.home.arpa`. Add the right ForwardAuth shape, make sure spoofed auth headers cannot slip through, and tell me what scope I should wire up in hlab-auth.
evals.expected_output: Uses the hlab-auth integration skill, classifies this as a Tier 1 Traefik ForwardAuth integration, keeps the stable X-Hlab-* contract, strips spoofed inbound headers, and documents the required hlab-auth scope and verification steps.

evals.prompt: I have an internal FastAPI dashboard behind Nginx. I want hlab-auth in front of it, and the app should auto-create a local profile from the trusted proxy identity. Please implement the right header handling and make sure direct requests without the proxy do not get treated as logged in.
evals.expected_output: Uses the hlab-auth integration skill, classifies the request as Tier 2 or Tier 3 depending on the repo shape, preserves the external-auth trust model, uses X-Hlab-User-Id as the durable linkage key, and fails closed when trusted headers or proxy provenance are missing.

evals.prompt: Can you set up my app to use hlab-auth as an OIDC provider so users can click Sign in with hlab-auth from the app login page? If that is not possible yet, show me the supported way to protect the app in v1 and what tradeoffs I should expect.
evals.expected_output: Uses the hlab-auth integration skill, explicitly identifies OIDC provider expectations as out of scope for hlab-auth v1, redirects to the supported proxy-header integration model, and explains the compatibility and trust-boundary tradeoffs clearly.

evals.prompt: I want an agent to manage only the Grafana app in hlab-auth after I approve it. Teach me the safest way to create a token for that agent, what scopes it should get, whether I should use a personal token or app token, and how the agent should call hlab-auth after that.
evals.expected_output: Uses the hlab-auth integration skill, recommends the least-privilege credential path, distinguishes app-bound service tokens from narrow personal tokens, gives the minimal scope set, notes current CLI versus API capabilities, and shows a safe usage and revocation workflow.
